Fix ImageMagick captions, add music, optimize render speed

  for captions, title, and sign watermark — no security policy errors
- Caption track: replace 33 individual ImageClips with a single VideoClip

  per-frame compositing overhead of N clips

- Sign watermark: render 3-letter abbreviation (ARI, SCO) instead of
  Unicode zodiac symbols unsupported by Montserrat
- Music: replace broken Pixabay CDN URLs with Kevin MacLeod / incompetech
  (CC BY 4.0) — verified working direct downloads
- OAuth: fix setup_youtube_auth.py for WSL/headless environments (no
  run_local_server, manually print URL and read code from stdin)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix dynamic connection starvation — new WS connections never started

_run_manager() loop called asyncio.wait(tasks, FIRST_COMPLETED) with
no timeout. _run_connection tasks run forever (stagger → connect →
infinite receive loop), so asyncio.wait blocked indefinitely. New
connections added dynamically via register_listener() (e.g., leader
monitor enabling auto-copy) were added to self._connections but never
got _run_connection tasks — the loop never re-scanned.

Fix: add timeout=10 to asyncio.wait so the loop wakes every 10s to
discover new connections. This is why leader 177 showed "Disconnected"
despite being registered — its SharedConnection existed but never
had a _run_connection task created for it.

commit.message:
Add tooltips to UI controls and implement service worker caching (#15)

* Fix mobile emblem cutoff on leaderboard champion banner

The emblem image was 180px x 180px on all screen sizes, but the container
was only 120px x 120px on mobile, causing it to be cut off. Updated the
image classes to be responsive:
- Mobile: 108px x 108px (to fit 120px container with 6px padding)
- Larger screens: 238px x 238px (to fit 250px container with 6px padding)

Fixes: Emblems get cut off on mobile

* Implement offline Service Worker caching for static assets

- Created Service Worker in src/sw/ with TypeScript support
- Added webpack build configuration for Service Worker bundling
- Integrated Service Worker registration via app/template.tsx
- Service Worker caches static assets and pages for offline use
- Added build scripts: build:sw:generate and build:sw
- Updated next.config.mjs and tsconfig.json for Service Worker support
- Installed webpack, ts-loader, and webpack-cli dependencies

The Service Worker will:
- Precache all static assets from .next directory on install
- Serve cached assets when offline
- Clean up old cache versions on activation
- Fall back to network when cache miss occurs

* Add generated Service Worker files to gitignore

Service Worker assets are auto-generated during build:
- src/sw/app-file-list.ts (generated file list for caching)
- src/sw/version.ts (synced from package.json)
- public/service-worker.js (bundled Service Worker)

These should not be committed since they're regenerated on each build.

* Remove auto-generated Service Worker files from git tracking

* Add tooltips to all controls on videos and lectures pages

Replaced basic title attributes with proper Radix UI tooltips for:

VideoSearchBar controls:
- Search clear button
- Thumbnail toggle button
- Favorites filter button
- Top picks toggle button
- Sort dropdown
- Season/Year filter dropdown
- Previous/Next page buttons

LecturesPage controls:
- Dictionary toggle button
- Dictionary search clear button

VideoCard (in grid):
- Favorite/save button

All tooltips now show helpful context on hover with proper positioning and styling.

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: Chat dropdown shows empty history for hookless sessions (#4)

Hookless sessions (proc-{pid}) have no hook_state and lsof doesn't
find session UUIDs (Claude opens/closes JSONL files). Added
find_newest_jsonl_for_cwd() that scans the project directory for the
most recently modified JSONL file as a last-resort fallback. Walks up
parent directories to handle worktree/child paths.

commit.message:
Fix SoS Total incorrectly falling below SoS Played and SoS Left (#9)

The old implementation averaged over the set-union of played and remaining
opponents. Weak played-only opponents (e.g. Browns at 30% PyPAT) were added
unweighted, which could drag Total below both components. Replace with a
game-count-weighted average: Total = (Played×games_played + Left×games_left)
/ total_games. Total is now always between Played and Left.

commit.message:
fix: lazy multer destination (use /tmp on Vercel, avoid eager mkdirSync)

multer diskStorage calls mkdirSync() in constructor when destination is
a string literal. On Vercel's read-only /var/task filesystem this throws
EROFS at module load time. Changed to a callback function so directory
is created per-request, using /tmp on serverless environments.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(infra): add S3 pack permissions to dashboard task role (#511)

The ECS task definition uses 'enkai-dashboard-task-dev' (legacy manual role)
but the IAM stack manages 'enkai-qualify-dev-task'. The grantReadWrite in CDK
was granting to a role that's never used. This adds an IAM::Policy resource
that attaches S3 pack bucket access to the actual dashboard task role.

Root cause: pack generation failing with "S3 bucket not found or inaccessible"
despite the bucket existing — the container simply lacked S3 permissions.

Co-authored-by: Developer <developer@frank.local>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add network request interception capabilities

Implemented 5 new network interception tools to enable blocking, modifying, and mocking network requests:

New Tools:
- network_interception_enable: Enable request interception on current page
- network_interception_disable: Disable interception and remove all rules
- network_interception_add_rule: Add rules to block/modify/mock requests
- network_interception_remove_rule: Remove specific interception rules
- network_interception_list_rules: List all active interception rules

Features:
- Block requests by URL pattern (e.g., ads, trackers, analytics)
- Modify request headers, method, and body
- Mock API responses with custom status, headers, and body
- Support for regex and substring URL matching
- Per-page interception state management

Use Cases:
- Test error handling by mocking failed API responses
- Block tracking scripts and ads during testing
- Modify authentication headers for testing
- Simulate different API responses without backend changes
- Test offline scenarios by blocking specific resources

Documentation:
- Updated README.md with new tool count (35 tools)
- Updated USAGE_GUIDE.md with network interception examples
- Added example prompts for API mocking and request blocking

Note: Video recording (screencast) was already implemented in the codebase.

README.md를 설계 문서로 교체, 문서 검증 반영

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add occupancy sensor accessory (#32)

* Add occupancy sensor accessory using Vision framework person detection

Implements #25: adds a new occupancy sensor that uses VNDetectHumanRectanglesRequest
on MonitoringCaptureSession frames to detect human presence, exposing a persistent
occupied/unoccupied state to HomeKit for automations.

- OccupancySensor: Vision-based detector running every ~2.5s on a dedicated queue
- HAPOccupancySensorAccessory: HAP service 0x86 with OccupancyDetected characteristic
- UI toggle + cooldown picker inside the camera card (1/2/5/10 min, default 5 min)
- Monitoring session auto-starts when occupancy enabled (even without HKSV armed)
- Keeps monitoring alive for occupancy when HKSV disarms

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix monitoring not resuming after snapshot when only occupancy sensor is active

The onSnapshotDidCapture handler only restarted monitoring when HKSV
recording was armed, leaving occupancy detection stalled after a
snapshot capture if HKSV wasn't active.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add confidence threshold and debug logging to occupancy sensor

Filter out false positives by requiring >= 0.5 confidence from Vision
person detection. Add per-check logging with confidence scores, cooldown
remaining, and detection state to aid debugging.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address PR feedback: reduce occupancy-only overhead and improve Vision usage

- Only wire motion/light detectors when HKSV recording is armed, not in
  occupancy-only mode (reduces CPU/battery in all three monitoring paths)
- Reuse VNDetectHumanRectanglesRequest instance instead of allocating per frame
- Wrap Vision execution in autoreleasepool for stable memory usage

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix formatting

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fetch 1-hour bars with extended=true and aggregate to daily candles

The 1day endpoint ignores the extended=true flag — it always returns
regular session OHLC (9:30am-4pm). To capture the full equity session
(4am-8pm ET), now fetches 1-hour bars with extended=true and
aggregates them into daily candles (group by date, max high, min low,
first open, last close). This is the same approach the intraday
analysis uses and is confirmed to include pre-market/after-hours data.
AVAV and similar tickers that expand outside in extended hours will
now correctly classify as X instead of A.

commit.message:
feat: promotion pipelines + AutoContext-Swarm bridge with multi-pass enrichment

Promotion workflows:
- promote-to-staging.yml: HeadyValidator → smoke test → Heady-Staging
- promote-to-main.yml: Manual gate + "PROMOTE TO PRODUCTION" confirmation
  → lint, test, security, docker validation → Heady-Main + semver tag

AutoContext-Swarm Bridge (autocontext-swarm-bridge.js):
- 5-pass enrichment before every action:
  1. Workspace scan  2. Latent space vector search
  3. Bee registry capabilities  4. Swarm topology awareness
  5. Cross-session pattern recall
- CSL-gated relevance filtering (φ-scaled thresholds)
- Latent space persistence for cross-session memory
- HeadyBee health/circuit-breaker state integration
- HeadySwarm pressure levels and task queue awareness
- executeGatedAction() wraps all AI actions with mandatory enrichment
- wrapWithMultiPassContext() utility for inline wrapping
